summary: Blues‑heavy early set with fiery guitar, solid bass, and Pigpen’s harp jams
blurb: The set bursts with blues‑driven numbers, highlighted by a scorching rendition of “I Know You Rider” and the tight King Bee > Caution jam. Pigpen’s harmonica leads the “Schoolgirl” sequence while Bill Kreutzmann drives the groove. The soundboard capture is crystal‑clear, making this one of the best early‑’66 tapes.
review:
Feb 25 1966 – Ivar Theater, Los Angeles – This early‑'60s Dead show captures a band in full blues stride. Ron “Pigpen” McKernan’s harp and gritty vocals dominate the opening numbers, especially on “Good Morning Little Schoolgirl” (though not listed) and the electrifying King Bee > Caution jam that showcases Phil’s adventurous bass lines. Jerry Garcia’s guitar is soaring throughout, with a particularly fiery solo on “I Know You Rider”. The set flows tight‑knit through classic folk‑rock numbers like “Cold Rain And Snow” and a seamless “Schoolgirl > You Don’t Love Me > Schoolgirl” sequence before closing with a punchy “Stealin’”. Bill Kreutzmann’s drumming is both masterful and propulsive, anchoring the band’s high‑energy performance. Both soundboard recordings are praised for crystal‑clear mix and minimal tape artifacts, making this one of the finest mid‑60s live documents.

For Deadheads seeking a pristine listening experience, the recording identified as gd66-02-25.sbd.unknown.1593.sbefail.shnf is recommended – it captures every nuance of Pigpen’s harmonica, Jerry’s tone, and Bill’s drive with exceptional clarity, ranking among the best SBD tapes from this era.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Both recordings are described with unequivocal positive language – ‘excellent’, ‘crystal‑clear’, ‘blazing early blues gems’, and multiple mentions of standout performances (Jerry’s fiery guitar, Pigpen’s harp, Phil’s adventurous bass). No negative comments appear across the analyses, indicating strong consensus that the show is musically exceptional.

best_recording.reason: Crystal‑clear soundboard with balanced mix, captures Pigpen’s harp and Jerry’s guitar perfectly; regarded as one of the finest mid‑60s Dead tapes.

key_highlights: Pigpen’s harmonica‑driven blues showcase, especially “Good Morning Little Schoolgirl” and the King Bee > Caution jam.
Jerry Garcia’s soaring tone and fiery solo on “I Know You Rider”.
Seamless “Schoolgirl > You Don't Love Me > Schoolgirl” sequence that keeps the energy flowing.
Recording quality is crystal‑clear, among the best SBD captures of the era.

band_performance.Jerry: Fiery, soaring guitar tone with a standout solo on “I Know You Rider”; overall playing is blazing and full of early‑'70 hints.
band_performance.Phil: Solid, adventurous bass work that adds depth to the King Bee > Caution jam and drives the low end throughout.
band_performance.Billy: Masterful drumming with a driving groove; provides powerful support for Pigpen’s harp and the band’s high‑energy flow.
